  intro hb
  have hiff : Binge BlackMirror ↔ Download BlackMirror := Rule2 BlackMirror BM_Netflix
  have hd : Download BlackMirror := (Iff.mp hiff) hb
  exact BM_not_download hd

axiom obj : Type

-- Entities

axiom Fluffy : obj
axiom OliveGarden : obj

axiom Pet : obj → Prop
axiom Cat : obj → Prop
axiom AllowedToMoveInWith : obj → obj → obj → Prop  -- person, building, pet
axiom WillRent : obj → obj → Prop                   -- person, building
axiom MonthlyRent : obj → Nat
axiom SecurityDeposit : obj → Nat
axiom RequiresDeposit : obj → Prop

-- Given facts
-- Pets are allowed in some managed buildings.
axiom some_managed_allows_pets : ∃ b : obj, Managed b ∧ AllowsPets b

-- A deposit is required to rent an apartment in a managed building.
axiom dep_required : ∀ b : obj, Managed b → RequiresDeposit b

-- The security deposit can be either equal to the monthly rent at a managed building or more (i.e., ≥).
axiom deposit_ge_rent : ∀ b : obj, Managed b → MonthlyRent b ≤ SecurityDeposit b

-- Fluffy is Tom's cat. Cats are pets.
axiom fluffy_cat : Cat Fluffy
axiom cats_are_pets : ∀ x : obj, Cat x → Pet x

-- The Olive Garden is a managed building.
axiom og_managed : Managed OliveGarden

-- The monthly rent at the Olive Garden is $2000.
axiom rent_og : MonthlyRent OliveGarden = 2000

-- $2000 is more than $1500.
axiom twoThousand_gt_1500 : 1500 < 2000

-- Tom will rent an apartment in a managed building if he is allowed to move in with Fluffy,
-- and the security deposit is no more than $1500.
axiom tom_rent_condition :
  ∀ b : obj, Managed b → (AllowedToMoveInWith Tom b Fluffy ∧ SecurityDeposit b ≤ 1500) → WillRent Tom b

-- If a managed building allows pets, then people are allowed to move in with a pet.
axiom allows_pets_implies_allowed_move_in :
  ∀ p b a : obj, Managed b → AllowsPets b → Pet a → AllowedToMoveInWith p b a

-- Useful derived fact: at Olive Garden, the deposit is > $1500 (since deposit ≥ rent = 2000 > 1500).
lemma deposit_gt_1500_og : 1500 < SecurityDeposit OliveGarden := by
  have h1 : MonthlyRent OliveGarden ≤ SecurityDeposit OliveGarden :=
    deposit_ge_rent OliveGarden og_managed
  have h2 : 1500 < MonthlyRent OliveGarden := by
    simpa [rent_og] using twoThousand_gt_1500
  exact Nat.lt_of_lt_of_le h2 h1

lemma not_deposit_le_1500_og : ¬(SecurityDeposit OliveGarden ≤ 1500) := by
  exact Nat.not_le.mpr deposit_gt_1500_og

-- We cannot derive ¬AllowedToMoveInWith Tom OliveGarden Fluffy from the given axioms,
-- since we do not have that Olive Garden disallows pets, and allowedness depends on pet policy, not deposit.
-- Cannot prove ¬AllowedToMoveInWith Tom OliveGarden Fluffy from given axioms.
</lean>

axiom dep_required : ∀ b : obj, Managed b → RequiresDeposit b

-- The security deposit can be either equal to the monthly rent at a managed building or more (i.e., ≥).
axiom deposit_ge_rent : ∀ b : obj, Managed b → MonthlyRent b ≤ SecurityDeposit b

-- Fluffy is Tom's cat. Cats are pets.
axiom fluffy_cat : Cat Fluffy
axiom cats_are_pets : ∀ x : obj, Cat x → Pet x

-- The Olive Garden is a managed building.
axiom og_managed : Managed OliveGarden

-- The monthly rent at the Olive Garden is $2000.
axiom rent_og : MonthlyRent OliveGarden = 2000

-- $2000 is more than $1500.
axiom twoThousand_gt_1500 : 1500 < 2000

-- Tom will rent an apartment in a managed building if he is allowed to move in with Fluffy,
-- and the security deposit is no more than $1500.

  ∀ b : obj, Managed b → (AllowedToMoveInWith Tom b Fluffy ∧ SecurityDeposit b ≤ 1500) → WillRent Tom b

  ∀ (p b a : obj), Managed b → AllowsPets b → Pet a → AllowedToMoveInWith p b a

-- Derived: at Olive Garden, the deposit is > $1500 (since deposit ≥ rent = 2000 > 1500).
lemma deposit_gt_1500_og : 1500 < SecurityDeposit OliveGarden := by
  have h1 : MonthlyRent OliveGarden ≤ SecurityDeposit OliveGarden :=
    deposit_ge_rent OliveGarden og_managed
  have h2 : 1500 < MonthlyRent OliveGarden := by
    simpa [rent_og] using twoThousand_gt_1500
  exact lt_of_lt_of_le h2 h1

lemma not_deposit_le_1500_og : ¬(SecurityDeposit OliveGarden ≤ 1500) := by
  exact not_le.mpr deposit_gt_1500_og

-- We cannot prove ¬AllowedToMoveInWith Tom OliveGarden Fluffy from the given axioms,
-- since we do not know whether OliveGarden allows pets.

  have hAlien : Alien Marvin := A2 Marvin hM
  have hEx : Extraterrestrial Marvin := A1 Marvin hAlien
  have hNotHuman : ¬Human Marvin := A3 Marvin hEx
  have hHuman : Human Marvin := A4 Marvin ⟨A5, hE⟩
  exact hNotHuman hHuman

<lean>
axiom obj : Type
axiom Marvin : obj

axiom Alien : obj → Prop
axiom Extraterrestrial : obj → Prop
axiom Human : obj → Prop
axiom FromEarth : obj → Prop
axiom FromMars : obj → Prop
axiom HighIntelligent : obj → Prop

-- All aliens are extraterrestrials.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Alien x → Extraterrestrial x
-- If someone is from Mars, then they are an alien.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, FromMars x → Alien x
-- No extraterrestrials are human.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Extraterrestrial x → ¬Human x
-- All highly intelligent beings from Earth are humans.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, HighIntelligent x ∧ FromEarth x → Human x
-- Marvin is a highly intelligent being.
axiom T1 : HighIntelligent Marvin
-- Marvin is either from Earth and from Mars, or he is from neither.
axiom T2 : (FromEarth Marvin ∧ FromMars Marvin) ∨ (¬FromEarth Marvin ∧ ¬FromMars Marvin)
-- If Marvin is not from Earth, then Marvin is an extraterrestrial.
axiom T3 : ¬FromEarth Marvin → Extraterrestrial Marvin

-- The statement to evaluate: ¬Human Marvin ∧ ¬FromMars Marvin
-- We can actually prove the statement is true from the premises:

theorem statement_true : ¬Human Marvin ∧ ¬FromMars Marvin := by
  -- First show Marvin cannot be both from Earth and from Mars
  have notEarthAndMars : ¬(FromEarth Marvin ∧ FromMars Marvin) := by
    intro h
    have hE : FromEarth Marvin := And.left h
    have hM : FromMars Marvin := And.right h
    have hAlien : Alien Marvin := A2 Marvin hM
    have hET : Extraterrestrial Marvin := A1 Marvin hAlien
    have notHum : ¬Human Marvin := A3 Marvin hET
    have hum : Human Marvin := A4 Marvin (And.intro T1 hE)
    exact notHum hum
  -- From the given disjunction and the impossibility of the first case, deduce neither Earth nor Mars
  have hneither : ¬FromEarth Marvin ∧ ¬FromMars Marvin := by
    cases T2 with
    | inl hboth =>
      exact False.elim (notEarthAndMars hboth)
    | inr hne =>
      exact hne
  have notEarth : ¬FromEarth Marvin := And.left hneither
  have notMars : ¬FromMars Marvin := And.right hneither
  -- From not Earth, Marvin is extraterrestrial, hence not human
  have et : Extraterrestrial Marvin := T3 notEarth
  have notHum : ¬Human Marvin := A3 Marvin et

axiom Celebrity : Person → Prop

axiom TaxHaven : Person → Prop

-- Everyone at the mixer is a Grand Slam champion or an Oscar-nominated actor.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : Person, At x → GrandSlam x ∨ OscarNom x
-- Every Grand Slam champion at the mixer is a professional tennis player.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : Person, At x ∧ GrandSlam x → ProTennis x
-- All Oscar-nominated actors at the mixer are celebrities.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : Person, At x ∧ OscarNom x → Celebrity x
-- All professional tennis players at the mixer are athletes.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : Person, At x ∧ ProTennis x → Athlete x
-- If a person at the mixer is a celebrity, then they are well paid.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : Person, At x ∧ Celebrity x → WellPaid x
-- If a person at the mixer is an athlete, then they are famous.
axiom A6 : ∀ x : Person, At x ∧ Athlete x → Famous x
-- All well-paid people at the mixer live in tax havens.
axiom A7 : ∀ x : Person, At x ∧ WellPaid x → TaxHaven x

axiom A8 : At Djokovic
-- If Djokovic is a famous athlete, then Djokovic is well-paid.
axiom A9 : Famous Djokovic ∧ Athlete Djokovic → WellPaid Djokovic

-- From the given context, we can derive that Djokovic lives in a tax haven.
theorem djokovic_tax_haven : TaxHaven Djokovic := by
  have hAt : At Djokovic := A8
  have hdisj : GrandSlam Djokovic ∨ OscarNom Djokovic := A1 Djokovic hAt
  cases hdisj with
  | inl hGS =>
      have hPro : ProTennis Djokovic := A2 Djokovic ⟨hAt, hGS⟩
      have hAth : Athlete Djokovic := A4 Djokovic ⟨hAt, hPro⟩
      have hFam : Famous Djokovic := A6 Djokovic ⟨hAt, hAth⟩
      have hWP : WellPaid Djokovic := A9 ⟨hFam, hAth⟩
      exact A7 Djokovic ⟨hAt, hWP⟩
  | inr hON =>
      have hCeleb : Celebrity Djokovic := A3 Djokovic ⟨hAt, hON⟩
      have hWP : WellPaid Djokovic := A5 Djokovic ⟨hAt, hCeleb⟩
      exact A7 Djokovic ⟨hAt, hWP⟩

-- Since we can prove TaxHaven Djokovic from the premises, we cannot prove its negation.
</lean>

axiom ClumsyFoodie : obj → Prop
axiom GoesOutFrequentlyForNewFood : obj → Prop

-- If people spill a lot of food on their clothing, then they are not notably tidy.
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, Spills x → ¬Tidy x
-- All clumsy foodies who go out frequently to find new food restaurants to try spill a lot of food.
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, ClumsyFoodie x ∧ GoesOutFrequentlyForNewFood x → Spills x
-- If people are cleanly, then they are notably tidy.
axiom R3 : ∀ x : obj, Cleanly x → Tidy x
-- If people value order and spotlessness, then they are cleanly.
axiom R4 : ∀ x : obj, ValuesOrder x → Cleanly x
-- All people whose families prioritize order and spotlessness value order and spotlessness.
axiom R5 : ∀ x : obj, FamilyPrioritizeOrder x → ValuesOrder x

-- Peter is either (spills ∧ cleanly) or (¬spills ∧ ¬cleanly)
axiom Pdisj : (Spills Peter ∧ Cleanly Peter) ∨ (¬Spills Peter ∧ ¬Cleanly Peter)

-- From the rules, (Spills ∧ Cleanly) leads to a contradiction (Tidy ∧ ¬Tidy).
theorem not_both : ¬(Spills Peter ∧ Cleanly Peter) := by
  intro h
  have hs : Spills Peter := h.left
  have hc : Cleanly Peter := h.right
  have ht : Tidy Peter := R3 Peter hc
  have hnt : ¬Tidy Peter := R1 Peter hs
  exact hnt ht

-- Hence the right disjunct must hold: ¬Spills ∧ ¬Cleanly for Peter.
theorem peter_not_spills_and_not_cleanly : ¬Spills Peter ∧ ¬Cleanly Peter := by
  have h := Pdisj
  have hna := not_both
  cases h with
  | inl hA => exact False.elim (hna hA)
  | inr hB => exact hB

<lean>
axiom obj : Type

-- Predicates on designs/styles
axiom ByMax : obj → Prop
axiom Adores : obj → Prop
axiom Zaha : obj → Prop
axiom Kelly : obj → Prop
axiom Brutalist : obj → Prop
axiom Interesting : obj → Prop
axiom Evocative : obj → Prop
axiom Dreamy : obj → Prop

-- All of Zaha Hadid's design styles that Max adores have interesting geometries.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Zaha x ∧ Adores x → Interesting x
-- No brutalist buildings that Max adores have interesting geometries.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Brutalist x ∧ Adores x → ¬Interesting x
-- Every style that Max adores is either Zaha Hadid's design style or Kelly Wearstler's design style.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Adores x → Zaha x ∨ Kelly x
-- All of Kelly Wearstler's design styles that Max adores are evocative.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Kelly x ∧ Adores x → Evocative x
-- All of Kelly Wearstler's design styles that Max adores are dreamy.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Kelly x ∧ Adores x → Dreamy x
-- If a design by Max that he adores has interesting geometries, then the design is a brutalist building and evocative.
axiom A6 : ∀ x : obj, ByMax x ∧ Adores x ∧ Interesting x → Brutalist x ∧ Evocative x

-- A helpful consequence: no design that is by Max, adored by Max, and interesting can exist.
theorem no_ByMax_adored_interesting : ∀ x : obj, ¬(ByMax x ∧ Adores x ∧ Interesting x) := by
  intro x
  intro h
  have bm : ByMax x := And.left h
  have ad : Adores x := And.left (And.right h)
  have ig : Interesting x := And.right (And.right h)
  have hb_ev := A6 x ⟨bm, ad, ig⟩
  have hb : Brutalist x := And.left hb_ev
  have notig : ¬Interesting x := A2 x ⟨hb, ad⟩
  exact notig ig

-- Target statement (existential reading): ∃x, ByMax x ∧ Evocative x ∧ Dreamy x
-- From the given axioms, there is no way to derive ¬∃x, ByMax x ∧ Evocative x ∧ Dreamy x.
-- We have no axioms forbidding a ByMax design from being both Evocative and Dreamy (e.g., without requiring Adores).
-- Hence we cannot prove the negation of the statement.
</lean>

axiom obj : Type
axiom Peter : obj

axiom Spill : obj → Prop
axiom Tidy : obj → Prop
axiom Clean : obj → Prop
axiom ValueOrder : obj → Prop
axiom FamilyPrioritizesOrder : obj → Prop
axiom ClumsyFoodie : obj → Prop
axiom GoesOutFrequently : obj → Prop

-- If people spill a lot of food on their clothing, then they are not notably tidy.
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, Spill x → ¬ Tidy x
-- All clumsy foodies who go out frequently are people who spill a lot of food on their clothing.
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, ClumsyFoodie x ∧ GoesOutFrequently x → Spill x
-- If people are cleanly, then they are notably tidy.
axiom R3 : ∀ x : obj, Clean x → Tidy x
-- If people value order and spotlessness, then they are cleanly.
axiom R4 : ∀ x : obj, ValueOrder x → Clean x
-- All people whose families prioritize order and spotlessness themselves value order and spotlessness.
axiom R5 : ∀ x : obj, FamilyPrioritizesOrder x → ValueOrder x
-- Peter is either (spills and cleanly) or (does not spill and not cleanly).
axiom R6 : (Spill Peter ∧ Clean Peter) ∨ (¬ Spill Peter ∧ ¬ Clean Peter)

-- Clean Peter leads to contradiction (hence ¬Clean Peter).
lemma clean_implies_false_for_Peter : Clean Peter → False := by
  intro c
  have disj := R6
  apply Or.elim disj
  · intro h
    have s : Spill Peter := And.left h
    have t : Tidy Peter := R3 Peter c
    have nt : ¬ Tidy Peter := R1 Peter s
    exact nt t
  · intro h
    have nc : ¬ Clean Peter := And.right h
    exact nc c

-- Hence Peter's family does not prioritize order and spotlessness.
theorem not_FamilyPrioritizesOrder_Peter : ¬ FamilyPrioritizesOrder Peter := by
  intro fpo
  have vo : ValueOrder Peter := R5 Peter fpo
  have c : Clean Peter := R4 Peter vo
  exact clean_implies_false_for_Peter c

-- Peter is not (a clumsy foodie who goes out frequently).
theorem not_CF_and_GO_Peter : ¬ (ClumsyFoodie Peter ∧ GoesOutFrequently Peter) := by
  intro hcfgo
  have s : Spill Peter := R2 Peter hcfgo
  -- Derive Clean Peter from R6 and s
  have disj := R6
  have c : Clean Peter := by
    apply Or.elim disj
    · intro h
      exact And.right h
    · intro h
      have ns : ¬ Spill Peter := And.left h
      exact False.elim (ns s)
  -- Now contradiction from s and c
  have t : Tidy Peter := R3 Peter c
  have nt : ¬ Tidy Peter := R1 Peter s
  exact nt t

-- Formalize the statement to evaluate:
-- Either (CF ∧ GO ∧ FPO) or (¬(CF ∧ GO) ∧ ¬FPO)
def Statement : Prop :=
  (ClumsyFoodie Peter ∧ GoesOutFrequently Peter ∧ FamilyPrioritizesOrder Peter) ∨
  ((¬ (ClumsyFoodie Peter ∧ GoesOutFrequently Peter)) ∧ (¬ FamilyPrioritizesOrder Peter))

-- In fact, the statement is provable from the premises (so its negation is not derivable).
theorem statement_true : Statement := by
  right
  constructor
  · exact not_CF_and_GO_Peter
  · exact not_FamilyPrioritizesOrder_Peter

-- Therefore, we cannot prove ¬Statement from the given axioms.
-- theorem statement_false : ¬Statement := by
--   -- Cannot be proven; the statement is actually true (see statement_true).
--   admit
</lean>

ANSWER: Failure

-- All dogs are animals that bark.

-- If an animal is terricolous, then it has legs.

-- KiKi is an animal.

  have hB : Barks KiKi := h.right
  have hNotLike : ¬LikesJane KiKi := H1 KiKi ⟨hA, hB⟩

axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, MakePlans x → Engaged x
-- All people who invite others to come to their ceremony make wedding plans.

-- Anyone who has a well-attended wedding invites others to come to their ceremony.

axiom A4 : ∃ x : obj, WellAttended x ∧ LargeFamily x
-- If John is engaged, then John does not invite friends to their ceremony and does not make wedding plans.
axiom A5 : Engaged John → (¬InviteFriends John ∧ ¬MakePlans John)
-- If John has a larger family, then either John has a well-attended wedding or invites friends to their ceremony.
axiom A6 : LargeFamily John → (WellAttended John ∨ InviteFriends John)

theorem john_not_well_attended : ¬WellAttended John := by
  intro hwa
  have hio : InviteOthers John := A3 John hwa
  have hmp : MakePlans John := A2 John hio
  have he  : Engaged John := A1 John hmp
  have hnotmp : ¬MakePlans John := (A5 he).right
  exact hnotmp hmp

-- If John has a larger family, then he invites friends (since the well-attended option is impossible).
theorem largefamily_implies_invitefriends : LargeFamily John → InviteFriends John := by
  intro hlf
  have h := A6 hlf
  cases h with
  | inl hwa => exact False.elim (john_not_well_attended hwa)
  | inr hif => exact hif

-- Cannot derive ¬LargeFamily John from the given axioms.
</lean>

  cases Bonnie_split with
  | inl h =>
      exact h.right
  | inr h =>
      have contra : False := h.left ae
      exact False.elim contra

axiom T4 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x ∧ Inactive x → Chaperone x

axiom BonnieEitherAttendStudent :
  (AttendEngaged Bonnie ∧ Student Bonnie) ∨ (¬AttendEngaged Bonnie ∧ ¬Student Bonnie)

theorem negate_statement :
  ¬ ((Chaperone Bonnie ∨ (¬Chaperone Bonnie → PerformOften Bonnie))
      → (YoungOrTeen Bonnie ∧ WishesFurtherAcademic Bonnie ∧ Inactive Bonnie)) := by

    | inr hInact =>
        have hCh : Chaperone Bonnie := T4 Bonnie (And.intro BonnieInClub hInact)
        exact False.elim (hNotCh hCh)

    intro hQ

    exact hNotStu hStu

  exact hNotQ hQ

  intro h
  have hC : Chemical Mixture := And.left h
  have hO : OnlyOneElement Mixture := And.right h
  have : ¬OnlyOneElement Mixture := A5 Mixture hC
  exact this hO

theorem mixture_not_chem_and_not_one : ¬Chemical Mixture ∧ ¬OnlyOneElement Mixture := by
  have hdisj := A6
  cases hdisj with
  | inl hboth =>
      have : False := mixture_not_pair hboth
      exact False.elim this
  | inr hneither =>
      exact hneither

theorem mixture_not_only_one : ¬OnlyOneElement Mixture := And.right mixture_not_chem_and_not_one

-- 2. All of the students with part-time jobs offered by the university currently work in the library.
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, Student x → HasPTJob x → WorksInLibrary x
-- 3. All of the students who are taking the database course are from the computer science department.
axiom R3 : ∀ x : obj, Student x → TakingDBCourse x → FromCS x
-- 4. All of the students taking a class with Professor David are taking the database course.
axiom R4 : ∀ x : obj, Student x → TakingWithDavid x → TakingDBCourse x
-- 5. All students working in the lab are taking a class with Professor David.
axiom R5 : ∀ x : obj, Student x → WorksInLab x → TakingWithDavid x

-- 6. James is a student working in the lab.
axiom TStudent : Student James
axiom TLab : WorksInLab James

-- 7. James doesn't work in the lab or have a part-time job offered by the university. (neither nor)
axiom NotLabJames : ¬WorksInLab James
axiom NotJobJames : ¬HasPTJob James

-- We prove that James does not have a part-time job
theorem james_no_ptjob : ¬HasPTJob James := by
  intro hJob
  have hLib : WorksInLibrary James := R2 James TStudent hJob
  have hNotCS : ¬FromCS James := R1 James TStudent hLib
  have hWithDavid : TakingWithDavid James := R5 James TStudent TLab
  have hDB : TakingDBCourse James := R4 James TStudent hWithDavid
  have hCS : FromCS James := R3 James TStudent hDB
  exact hNotCS hCS

-- If something is skittish, then it is not calm.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Skittish x → ¬Calm x
-- All the squirrels that can be spotted near the campus are skittish.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, (Squirrel x ∧ Spotted x) → Skittish x
-- Rockie can be spotted near the campus, and it is calm.
axiom A6 : Spotted Rockie ∧ Calm Rockie

  -- From "only spotted are rabbits or squirrels"
  have hDisj : Rabbit Rockie ∨ Squirrel Rockie := A3 Rockie hSpotted
  -- Rockie cannot be a squirrel (would be skittish, contradicting calm)
  have hRabbit : Rabbit Rockie := by
    cases hDisj with

    | inr hSq =>
      have hSk : Skittish Rockie := A5 Rockie (And.intro hSq hSpotted)
      have hNotCalm : ¬Calm Rockie := A4 Rockie hSk
      exact False.elim (hNotCalm hCalm)
  -- Therefore Rockie is cute
  have hCute : Cute Rockie := A1 Rockie (And.intro hRabbit hSpotted)
  -- Now negate the implication
  intro hImp
  have hConsequent : Skittish Rockie ∧ Turtle Rockie := hImp (And.intro hCute hCalm)
  have hSk : Skittish Rockie := And.left hConsequent
  exact hNotSkittish hSk
</lean>

lean_code:
axiom obj : Type

-- Entities
axiom Tom : obj
axiom Fluffy : obj
axiom OliveGarden : obj
axiom Dollars1500 : obj
axiom Dollars2000 : obj

-- Predicates and functions
axiom Managed : obj → Prop
axiom Pet : obj → Prop
axiom Cat : obj → Prop
axiom AllowsPets : obj → Prop
axiom AllowedMoveInWith : obj → obj → obj → Prop   -- person, pet, building
axiom WillRentIn : obj → obj → Prop                -- person, building
axiom MonthlyRent : obj → obj                      -- building → amount
axiom SecDeposit : obj → obj                       -- building → amount
axiom Greater : obj → obj → Prop                   -- amount > amount
axiom Le : obj → obj → Prop                        -- amount ≤ amount
axiom DepositRequired : obj → Prop                 -- building has a required deposit

-- Facts from the context
axiom Cats_are_pets : ∀ x : obj, Cat x → Pet x
axiom Fluffy_is_cat : Cat Fluffy
axiom some_managed_allow_pets : ∃ b : obj, Managed b ∧ AllowsPets b
axiom OliveGarden_managed : Managed OliveGarden
axiom rent_OG_2000 : MonthlyRent OliveGarden = Dollars2000
axiom two_thousand_more_than_1500 : Greater Dollars2000 Dollars1500

-- If a managed building allows pets, then people are allowed to move in with a pet.
axiom allow_move_in_if_allows_pets :
  ∀ b : obj, Managed b → AllowsPets b → ∀ p pet : obj, Pet pet → AllowedMoveInWith p pet b

-- A deposit is required to rent an apartment in a managed building.
axiom deposit_required_to_rent :
  ∀ p b : obj, Managed b → WillRentIn p b → DepositRequired b

-- The security deposit can be either equal to the monthly rent at a managed building or more.
axiom deposit_eq_or_more_than_rent :
  ∀ b : obj, Managed b → (SecDeposit b = MonthlyRent b ∨ Greater (SecDeposit b) (MonthlyRent b))

-- Tom will rent an apartment in a managed building if allowed with Fluffy and deposit ≤ $1500.
axiom tom_rents_if_allowed_and_affordable :
  ∀ b : obj, Managed b → AllowedMoveInWith Tom Fluffy b → Le (SecDeposit b) Dollars1500 → WillRentIn Tom b

-- From the given axioms, we cannot derive ¬(WillRentIn Tom OliveGarden).

-- Hence we cannot prove the negation of the statement.
